title: NOC 11101 – Financial and Investment Analysts | GTR Immigration
description: Financial and Investment Analysts classified under NOC 11101 are among the most in-demand finance professionals in Canada’s immigration system. These specialists support corporate strategy, capital planning, portfolio management, and risk assessment—making them strong candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Professionals in this occupation may perform some or all of the following:
Evaluate financial risks and prepare forecasts, capital plans, and recommendations
Analyze investment projects and corporate-financing scenarios
Advise on tendering and contract financing
Track financing initiatives with lenders and investors
Develop tools for portfolio and treasury analysis
Prepare risk profiles for debt portfolios
Assist in operating and capital budgets
Research companies, equities, bonds, and market trends
Analyze financial statements, yields, and valuation metrics
Prepare industry and economic outlook reports
Provide portfolio recommendations to executives, fund managers, and advisors
Draft briefing notes and analytical correspondence
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in commerce, business administration, finance, accounting, or economics
MBA or master’s degree in finance (sometimes required)
Professional credentials such as CFA, CFP, CIM, or CPA (often preferred or required)
Treasury certifications such as CTP for some roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to generate 23,700 new positions for Financial and Investment Analysts, while approximately 29,700 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—especially applicants from India—this highlights the importance of a carefully planned immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Financial and Investment Analysts continue to experience strong hiring demand across Canada’s financial-services sector, corporate-finance teams, banks, insurers, and investment firms. Growth in capital markets and regulatory complexity after the pandemic has further increased the need for analytical expertise.
Excellent immigration opportunities for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings commonly range from $46,200 to $110,900, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Why Financial & Investment Analysts Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 11101 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
